entry.title: Unlock new levels of productivity with AI in SAP Build
entry.content:
Why do we need more AI in SAP Build?

For a very simple reason: our users want to be even more efficient in delivering innovations and SAP extensions. Our customers want to accelerate application development and process automation with the help of AI to get more time to focus on what really matters instead of spilling time on tedious and repetitive tasks.

Let me outline some of the problems our customers describe:

  • “As a business process expert, I want to get assistance to automate my processes and critical workflow much faster”.
  • “I’m an application developer, and I'd like to stop spending time on tedious tasks like creating data models and sample data to test my apps. And if AI could help me create some basic JavaScript code, not rocket science, that would be great!”
  • “For a software developer like me, it would be great if we could efficiently code applications and concentrate on the value-add coding part, so I can deliver on my projects faster”

At SAP, we’re committed to delivering AI capabilities that make it even better to develop with SAP Build. Just a month ago, we announced the general availability of our Joule copilot for ABAP developers: now Joule speaks ABAP!  Today we proudly announced new Joule capabilities for developers. In the next few paragraphs, I will take you on a deep dive into the new capabilities you can leverage to benefit from the power of (generative) Artificial Intelligence for building applications or automating business processes.

First, we identified a few key themes where AI has a significant impact in the realm of app development and process automation:

  • Application Generation: Generate CAP & RAP applications from scratch including data models, sample data, unit tests and more
  • Code Optimization: Generate and optimize JavaScript, Fiori/UI5 code with predictive code completion, code search, and code explanation
  • Artifacts Generation: Generate and summarize complex artifacts such as processes, data types, scripts, decisions, as well as approval recommendations

All these capabilities significantly accelerate application development and process automation and – let’s be honest – make it more fun, also.

Now let’s explore together how AI will assist you across all SAP Build.

SAP Build Process Automation

The purpose of AI and generative AI capabilities in SAP Build Process Automation is very simple yet powerful: assisting our customers in the implementation of their process automations. For that, the design capabilities leverage generative AI to allow users to interactively generate and edit artifacts from natural language description, as well as generating summaries of existing artifacts.

With that, we create immediate value for our customers:

  • Building artefacts such as processes, forms and business rules, and automating processes with the help of AI based on natural language input help increase developer productivity, improve quality and enable further innovations.

  • Summarizing processes, workflows and complex artefacts such as Business Decisions (Rules) using natural business language enables a quicker handover and reduced time for documenting processes.

We also added two additional AI-based capabilities that make process automation better:

  • DOX Premium service is integrated with SAP Build bringing a paradigm shift in document processing automation world. Generative Al-based document understanding not only integrates cutting-edge natural language processing capabilities but also holds the promise of eliminating the necessity for the laborious and costly endeavor of template creation and maintenance.
  • SAP Task Center's intelligent approval recommendation capability enables faster and better decisions based on machine learning, streamlining operations, and improving efficiency with AI-driven insights based on patterns observed in other approval tasks.

To get a concrete view on how AI works in SAP Build Process Automation, watch our 7 minutes video demo on Intelligent Processing of Sales Orders with Enterprise Automation.

SAP Build Apps

There is also a new AI-based capability in our no-code development environment. In SAP Build Apps we now have UI generation from data. The UI of an application is often directly related to the structure of the data handled by the application, so AI can propose Us based on data. In the screenshot below you can see data entities that are part of the application – in this case, Books, Authors and Genres – and right next to these, the UI pages were created by AI.

In order to activate this Generative AI capability in SAP Build Apps, you have to 

  1. Click on the "Generative AI" tile in your SAP Build tenant configuration:

  2. Enable Generative AI feature with the toggle:



SAP Build Code

Many AI capabilities have been introduced during the last months in SAP Build Code. Let me share a recap with you:

Joule supports complex multi-steps tasks for advanced CAP application generation

As of early 2025, the optimal approach for professional developers working with Generative AI involves creating project skeletons that cover general requirements, followed by incremental enhancements. SAP Build Code Joule expands on this practice with multi-tasks support, allowing developers to use a single prompt to engage specialized agents for various tasks. By coordinating these agents, Joule creates more comprehensive project foundations, thereby streamlining the development process. This approach enables developers to focus more on refining and customizing their applications, improving productivity and efficiency.

As an example, when a developer asks Joule for assistance in creating an application, an execution plan is displayed. The complex requirements are broken down into subtasks to generate

  1. the data model
  2. the sample data
  3. the application logic

and we apply our best practices for each step. The developer can review the improved user requirements and enhance them manually, via the pencil button.

For more details, read our blog post SAP Build Code Joule Evolves: Unveiling Multi-Agent Support for Advanced CAP Application Generation.

Joule for mobile app development

Joule now also supports mobile app development in SAP Build Code: it uses mobile services, like MDK action generation.

Find more in this blog post from Jitendra Kansal: “Streamline SAP MDK Development with the Power of Generative AI and Joule” and for additional details, read this technical documentation. 

Joule for searching and analyzing existing code

With Joule copilot, you can now use natural language queries to quickly and easily locate specific code snippets. You can simply ask Joule what you're looking for, and it will instantly show you the files relevant to your search criteria, saving you time and effort in your coding journey.

For more details, read our blog post Product Updates for SAP Build Code – October 2024 Edition.

Joule for SAP HANA Machine Learning

The SQL console provided by the SAP HANA Database Explorer extension in SAP Build Code supports SQL statement generation via natural language. Users can enter a text prompt and use the new run method “Run Prompt” to generate a SQL statement that can be executed on the SAP HANA Cloud database. The generated statement can be executed like any other statement directly from the SQL console.

For more details, read our blog post Product Updates for SAP Build Code – October 2024 Edition

Joule for Fiori tools and UI5 guided development

SAP Build Code also offers a variety of generative AI functionalities to support UI5 development. Joule can now be used to both accelerate the development and provide support for those who may not be UI5 experts.

For more details, read our blog posts UI5 freestyle development in SAP Build Code with Joule and SAPUI5 Meets TypeScript: A Smooth Migration Journey with Joule. 

The business user view: SAP Build Work Zone and AI

Of course, beyond the developer, we also investigate how we can make the SAP Build business user more efficient with the help of AI. So, while the below update is not part of our Joule for Developers capabilities, I still wanted to mention this and provide our readers the chance to dive deeper.

Joule is now integrated into SAP Build Work Zone Standard Edition, enhancing the workspace with AI-powered capabilities

This enables users to interact with Joule and get answers from all connected systems​. An example would be that you're trying to track the status of a purchase order. Instead of navigating through multiple screens, you simply ask Joule, “Has my purchase order been approved?”. Joule instantly provides the status and key details directly within its window in SAP Build Work Zone, this seamless interaction eliminates the need to switch between applications, saving time and effort.

For more examples of how Joule can transform your workspaces, read @Yumnahabbasi's blog post: “Joule is Now Integrated into SAP Build Work Zone!”

Feedback from Early Adopters: Up to 40% cost reduction!

Now, back to the developer view. We gathered feedback from beta users to understand the impact these capabilities have on their application development and process automation.  The results they achieved in terms of productivity speak for themselves:

  • Up to 40%​ cost reduction for ERP extension migration: it enables customers to review and transition existing extensions faster and more efficiently into the cloud. It simplifies future upgrades by providing side-by-side extensions that keep the ERP core clean
  • Up to 30% reduction in low-code process automation effort: building and automating processes based on natural language input, allows for increased developer productivity, improved quality and more innovations.
  • Up to 30%​ cost reduction for pro-code application development: generating code based on natural-language input and pre-trained code, and data generation model, allows for faster development, quicker migration to newer digital environments and improved development capacity for innovations.
  • Up to 10% time savings for process developers: summarizing processes and complex artefacts using natural language enables a quicker handover and reduced time for understanding processes.
  • Up to 10% faster time to market for new low-code applications: empowering developers to build applications and automate processes faster based on natural language input accelerates projects.

Join our Webcast

We've set up a webcast where I'll have the pleasure to be showing you AI in SAP Build in action, plus you'll have the chance to bring me and other product managers any questions you might have.

Join us on April 10, 2025!

  • APJ: 2:00 PM SGT (Singapore) / 4:00 PM AEST (Sydney)
  • EMEA: 2:00 PM CET (Central Europe) / 1:00 PM GMT (UK)
  • NA: 2:00 PM EST / 11:00 AM PST (Pacific)

You can also

  • Watch our Joule for Developers video
  • Attend our LinkedIn Live event on March 25th at 9AM PST
  • Participate in our SAP Community contest 
  • Check out the official FAQs on Joule for developers
  • Attend our SAP Community Ask Me Anything session in April (link to come soon)

Enjoy AI and Joule in SAP Build!

entry.title: Enhancing Business Agility with Enterprise Automation: SAP Build and SAP Signavio
entry.content:
The ability to intelligently automate business processes is top of mind for many stakeholders involved in process management across organizations. According to a recent IDC study, annual spending on Intelligent Process Automation (IPA) technologies is projected to reach $102 billion by 2028. Of this, $19.9 billion will be allocated to AI-powered automation tools, with an impressive annual growth rate of 43.4%.

While there will be certainly no shortage in resources to spend for automation software, the real challenge remains in the details: How can organizations implement a holistic approach for process excellence, which allows them to manage processes, identify bottlenecks, deliver scalable automations and integrate their process extension within a holistic process landscape. In the previous blogpost, @Manuel_Namyslo has already outlined in detail how SAP’s framework on Enterprise Automation delivers an solution to the problem statement mentioned above.

And this blogpost series will offer you a more detailed guidance on the interconnectivity of each service included in Enterprise Automation which are:

  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Integration Suite
  • and, of course, SAP Signavio.

Manuel has already published a blog on the joint usage patterns for SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite in the first blogpost of the series.

Let’s explore the value you can unlock for your processes by combining SAP Build Process Automation with SAP Signavio in this one.

What is Enterprise Automation?

Enterprise automation with SAP software provides an integrated set of tools for process mining, automation, integration, monitoring, and optimization of your processes and workflows. It helps you quickly and intuitively gain insights into your business processes and identify the right automation tool for the right outcome. While each solution can be leveraged independently, Enterprise Automation offer native integration between SAP Build, SAP integration Suite and SAP Signavio – supplemented with best practices, guidance and pre-build content which can be implemented directly.

  • SAP Signavio provides visibility into business processes and potential bottlenecks, root-cause analysis, and suggestions for improvement based on 200,000 industry benchmarks and best practices. This detailed understanding of inefficiencies across your organization enables you to optimize and automate processes.
  • SAP Integration Suite is an integration platform as a service with thousands of prebuilt integrations, APIs, events and connectors to SAP and third-party software applications. The suite reduces complexity across on-premise and multi-cloud environments, helping you optimize comprehensive business processes.
  • SAP Build to create automations and applications using the low-code and no-code design tools. Including pre-built automations and connectors for SAP software and third-party systems, the solutions let your business experts rapidly address process inefficiencies.

Leverage the combined power of SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Signavio

1. Process Recommendations

Every organization runs complex business processes, from procurement to order management and HR workflows. However, inefficiencies, bottlenecks, and compliance gaps often remain hidden within these processes, leading to delays and unnecessary costs.

SAP Signavio Process Insights is a lightweight solution designed to help businesses analyze and optimize their operational processes. It provides data-driven insights by identifying inefficiencies, automation opportunities, as well as performance bottlenecks, and can be leveraged alongside your SAP S/4HANA data. The tool enables companies to benchmark their processes against industry best practices, prioritize improvement initiatives, and accelerate digital transformation. By offering predefined process performance indicators and process recommendations, SAP Signavio Process Insights helps organizations enhance efficiency, reduce costs, and drive continuous improvement in their business operations.

However, identifying issues is just the first step—organizations need a way to act on these insights and implement improvements. This is where SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and SAP Build come into play.

SAP Signavio Process Insights can recommend specific pre-built content, tailored to the process specifics. This content is available to be deployed with SAP Build Process Automation, so companies can directly implement these packages to make their scenarios executable.

2. From Insights to Action

Having the recommendations of what can be used is a great option to start. But there is more to the story. Organizations need a seamless way to make the outcomes of the process analysis actionable and continuously optimize their operations. This is where SAP Signavio Process Intelligence and SAP Build Process Automation come together to drive meaningful transformation.

SAP Signavio Process Intelligence enables organizations to perform a full-fledged process mining, including:

  • Analysis process data extracted from SAP S/4HANA, SAP ECC, and other connected systems.
  • Detection of inefficiencies, such as approval delays, reworks needed or missed SLAs.
  • Visualization of process flows with its deviations, conformance and variants.

This insight provides a clear roadmap for improvement—but how do we act on it efficiently?

SAP Build Process Automation bridges the gap by enabling organizations to implement improvements with minimal IT effort.

Using the combination of the tools, businesses can define conditions and triggers based on findings from SAP Signavio Process Intelligence and trigger an approval flow or a task automation scenario (e.g. an exception flow) directly using the native API connectivity.

For example, if Process Intelligence detects a high percentage of purchase orders stuck in approval, SAP Build Process Automation can automatically trigger a workflow that escalates delays, notifies the right stakeholders, or reroutes tasks to speed up processing.

Now, optimization is not a one-time effort—it requires continuous monitoring and refinement. Once automated workflows are in place, organizations must track their performance and fine-tune processes as needed.

With SAP Signavio Process Intelligence, businesses can:

  • Measure the impact of automation by tracking KPIs before and after implementation.
  • Identify new bottlenecks or areas for further automation in a cycle of continuous improvement, receiving the data from the executed workflow.
  • Refine processes dynamically, ensuring agility and efficiency in ever-changing business conditions.

3. Model-to-Execute: BPMN Import for Executable Processes

In today's dynamic business environment, organizations need to rapidly transform business processes from concept to execution. One of the key challenges has been the disconnect between process modeling and process automation—where business analysts model processes that then need to be separately implemented by technical teams, often leading to misalignment and delays.

SAP is looking to address this challenge through a BPMN Import capability, a roadmap item for SAP Build Process Automation, bridging the gap between process design and execution.

Traditionally, business processes follow this journey:

  1. Business analysts model best practice processes, what can be done using SAP Signavio Process Manager
  2. These models are handed off to technical teams
  3. Technical teams re-interpret and rebuild these models in execution platforms
  4. Discrepancies emerge between what was designed and what was implemented

This handoff creates friction, delays implementation, and often results in execution that doesn't fully align with the original vision.

The ability to import models from SAP Signavio Process Manager directly into SAP Build Process Automation transforms this approach. Now, the BPMN models created by business users can serve as the actual foundation for executable automation.

In addition, it is important to outline that SAP Build Process Automation will be supporting generic BPMN models import, allowing to upload BPMN diagrams from different solutions.

Please expect more details on this after the feature would be released.

Conclusion

The joint usage of SAP Signavio and SAP Build offers a powerful combination for businesses looking to optimize their processes and drive efficiency. By leveraging product recommendations, direct integration for automation, and model-to-execute capabilities, organizations can quickly identify, automate, and execute process improvements.

Please find some useful links below:

  • Official Enterprise Automation with SAP page 
  • How to get started with Enterprise Automation Guide 
  • Demo: Experience Enterprise Automation with SAP for Procure-to-Pay Processes
  • Whitepaper: Transition to SAP S/4HANA with Enterprise Automation  
  • Enterprise Automation with SAP Product Tour  
  • Enterprise Automation with SAP webinar

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation - How to transition to Shared Environment - getting started
entry.content:
Dear Community,

Couple of months ago we introduced the Shared Environment for SAP Build Process Automation. We described the main benefits compared to the public Environment here and here. We monitor the usage on a monthly basis and see millions of automation jobs and process instances which are now leveraging the Shared Environments.

Let's recap four important benefits:

  • Project-version deployed within a shared environment is always the latest active;
  • Dependencies within a specific shared environment are always the latest and consistent;
  • A powerful sharing capability and privilege definition;
  • Enhanced security with API calls.

Project-version deployed within a shared environment is always the latest active

After deploying a version, the previous version is non-longer "deployed". Previous version non-longer used can then easily deleted from the Lobby.

Let's assume you have a project with 10 versions. The version 1.0.8 is deployed and the version 1.0.9 should be deployed. While deploying this version the previous version 1.0.8 is non-longer activated. Existing tasks - if any - are gracefully finished. If needed (cleaning) you can easily delete the version 1.0.8 which is unused.

 

 

 

When undeploying a project within a shared environment, the workflow definition remains deployed... but inactive. Keeping the history of past instances.

Dependencies within a specific shared environment are always the "latest" and consistent

  • Typical scenario:
    • Let's assume a project A is deployed with a dependent project D1 with version 1.0.40
    • Let's assume a project B is deployed with the same dependent project D1 with version 1.0.42
    • Project A will automatically leverage the dependent project D1 with the version 1.0.42
  • The semantic versioning works for the minor and patch:
    • When attempting to deploy a project C with the same dependent project D1 with the version 2.0.0, deployment will be blocked until the project A and B are upgraded with the version 2.X.X
    • Deploying this project in another Shared Environment is however doable

A powerful sharing capability and privilege definition

A Shared Environment comes with different privileges. By default, a ProcessAutomationDeveloper CANNOT see a Shared Environment by default. All content deployed are not only isolated, but the user cannot see until the Shared Environment admin enables it:

  • Monitor is the minimum right for allowing others to see the content deployed and the jobs
  • View logs is the right for allowing users to see the "context", inputs/outputs jobs
  • Deploy is the right for allowing users to deploy within a shared environment
  • Administrate is the right for allowing users to all tasks mentioned before and the ability to share to others
  • Execute - for enabling the execution of workflow or Decision or Form access
  • Supervise - for enabling the Process Visibility Scenario Dashboard access

Finally, agents belonging to a shared environment will take care ONLY to the projects deployed in this specific shared environment.

With the ProcessAutomationDelegate, you can have dedicated users focusing on monitoring only for example without the ability to create new project.

Enhanced security with API calls and technical user (API-Key)

For calling a process deployed within a shared environment you'll need to use the extra parameter environmentId (leveraging the environment identifier).

API Key is an extra layer of security. There are different rights to fine tune while creating an API Key. API Key requires ProcessAutomationAdmin for security reasons.

How to deploy a project?

  1. First thing to do is to create your Shared Environment and add some users within. Choose carefully the identifier: When promoting the content from a development tenant to a production tenant you might want to keep the name identical across tenants.
  2. From the Studio, click Deploy and you should be able to choose the corresponding Shared Environment
  3. Once deployed the content is now available and can be called

How to start a workflow definition deployed within a shared environment?

Compared to a process deployed within the public environment, an explicit API trigger is needed. Once created for starting in an instance you will need to provide:

  • environmentId as query
  • api-key

Once done, calling APIs will be doable.

Can I deploy within a shared environment AND public environment?

Yes, you can deploy a project within a Shared Environment while keeping the public environment.

You can deploy the same project or different project-version:

  • Project A with version 1.0.41 is deployed within Shared Environment E1
  • Project A with version 1.0.42 is deployed within Shared Environment E2
  • Project A with version 1.0.21 is still deployed within Public Environment

Can I undeploy?

Please note that undeploying a project with Process within the Public Environment will still undeploy and remove all past instances. On a development tenant:

  • Undeploy the project-version "active" within the public environment first to start "clean"
    • All versions will be also undeployed if there is a process
  • Deploy the project within a shared environment

How to undeploy inactive project-versions?

Within the Public Environment, activate the switch "Show inactive versions" for displaying all previous and inactive versions:

Why no longer use Public Environment?

  • All projects deployed within the Public Environment are accessible for the tenant users. That means a user who knows a form url can trigger it.
  • All versions deployed for a given project are deployed "for ever". Cleaning old versions might take more time.
  • When using automation and agents, the jobs are assigned with the agent pool. Shared Environment is more efficient for that end and robust compared to agent attributes.
  • Public Environment will be deprecated and after a transition period:
    • New project won't be allowed to be deployed
    • New project-version won't be allowed to be deployed
    • New jobs won't be allowed to be launched

entry.title: 🎉SAP Build Brings Generative AI to Process Automation to enable your Business Processes
entry.content:
Try now in your SAP BTP Trial, Free Tier, Global Account - SAP Build Generative AI

 

Enabling Generative AI

Enable Generative AI in Control Tower for automated recommendations, enhanced efficiency, and time savings. Use it to create and summarize complex business processes, analyze data for informed decisions, and elevate app development. 

Business Context

SAP Build offers the following Generative AI functionality to enhance your business operations. You have the flexibility to enable or disable the Generative AI feature at the tenant level.
  • Automatically Create and Summarize Complex Business Processes: Outline workflows, generate step-by-step instructions, and ensure clear and efficient operations.

  • Use AI to Analyze Data and Generate Informed Decisions: Create a summary of decisions, get action recommendations actions, provide insights, and enable better decisions across your organization.

  • Streamline Your Data Collection with AI-Generated Forms: Automatically create forms tailored to your specific needs, ensuring accuracy and saving you time creating and managing forms.
  • Elevate Your App Development Process with AI-Driven UI Generation: Get help to create user-friendly interfaces with layout, component, and style suggestions, helping you build applications faster and more efficiently.

How to enable Generative AI in SAP Build

  1. From the left navigation menu, choose Control Tower.
  2. In the Tenant Configuration, choose Generative AI.

  3. Use toggle switch to enable Generative AI and accept the AI Usage Terms and Conditions.

SAP Build Process Automation - Usage of Generative AI

You can now use generative artifical intelligence in SAP Build Process Automation to generate and edit business processes, generate business rules, generate new code in a scipt task, and generate certain artifacts.

You can use generative AI to do the following:
  • Generate a business process, a data type, or a form from the Overview screen of your project. See Generate a Data Type and Generate a Form.
  • Edit or add steps to a process. See Generate a Process.
  • Generate new code in a script task. See Generate a Script using Generative AI in a Script Task
  • Explain a decision or generate business rules. See Use Generative AI in a Decision.

To use these features you need to enable generative AI for your tenant. See Generative AI.

The generative AI features are only supported on the following data centers:
  • Americas - US10, US30
  • EMEA - EU10, EU11, EU20
  • Asia and Australia - AP10

Note that some restrictions and limitations apply.

Known Limitations for Generative AI

When using generative AI, note the following limitations:
  • You can only use generative AI in the following cases:
    • You can generate a business process and explain a business process. It is also possible to edit a process and add some process steps. Note that it is not possible to generate branches, go-to steps, or map data.
    • You can generate a form
    • You can generate a data type
    • You can generate new code in a script task
    • You can explain a decision and generate business rules
  • Artifacts that are not listed here are not supported.
  • You cannot edit the content of artifacts, with the exception of the process.
  • You cannot release, deploy or delete a process using generative AI.
  • When using generative AI in the script editor, you can only generate code using JavaScript version ES 5.1.

Generative AI

Area Resource Quota
Tenant LevelAI requests for proceses1000 / month
 AI requests for business rules1200 / month

Generative AI - Model Limits

Element Name Property Name Limit
Process or ArtifactPrompt Description5 to 1000 characters
ProcessProcess Summarization

100 nodes

500 links between nodes

Restrictions, Quotas, Limits - Reference Documentation

entry.title: How to Enable Joule Capabilities in SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
SAP recently announced some new Joule capabilities for developers within SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Apps. If you’re wondering how to enable these capabilities in your workspace, you’re in the right place.

In this post, we’ll look at the steps to undertake to enable the AI capabilities. But first, let’s just recap the announcement and the various recent posts:

  • Joule for developers, announced on March 18, is a set of AI capabilities enabled by Joule across SAP Build and ABAP developer tools.
  • Read the announcement blog here
  • Read the technical blog here
  • Read the FAQs on Joule for developer here

Now, lets look at the steps to enable the AI capabilities in SAP Build:

1. On the Build homepage, click Control Tower in the left-hand navigation panel.

2. Select Generative AI from the settings canvas.

3. Toggle the Enable AI button

 

4. Read and accept the SAP AI terms

5. Verify that the AI features are enabled

Availability and Licensing

Joule capabilities for developers are currently available across SAP Build Code, ABAP, SAP Build Process Automation, and SAP Build Apps.

Good news! Now through the end of June, we’re making Joule capabilities for developers accessible for free to all developers using SAP Build and ABAP Development Tools. For all SAP Build development tools, this free period extends until the end of June 2025. The ABAP AI capabilities will be available for free until August 2025. The only requirement to take advantage of this free offer is for the organization/user to sign the SAP AI Terms. Once this free offer concludes, the commercial model based on AI Units will take effect.

Note: You will still need the license to use the underlying SAP Build solutions and ABAP to utilize the AI capabilities within those tools.

Start leveraging the power of AI today! Enable Joule for developers in your SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Apps environments and experience a significant boost in your development efficiency. Don't miss out on the free offer available for a limited time!

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation - ProcessAutomationDelegate explained (Security and Roles too)
entry.content:
Dear Community,

Couple of months ago we introduced the ProcessAutomationDelegate. Let's find out what it brings for Governance among other Role Collections.

Typical Tasks

While setting your tenant, questions can quickly come for knowing WHO is the admin and WHO is not. Admin can do EVERYTHING within SAP Build Process Automation. Here an overview of the different tasks assigned by user profile with the corresponding Role Collections:

ProcessAutomationDelegate?

In a nutshell, ProcessAutomationDelegate is like a ProcessAutomationDeveloper without the right adding a project or add anything without being granted in a container such as Project or Environment. This role collection is convenient when:

  • you need to assign a user to monitor instances and jobs execution
  • you need to assign a user to a specific project without giving the opportunity to create a new project
  • you need to assign a user to a specific environment without giving too much responsibility

Combined with Shared Environment Privileges, you can customize your governance rules.

  • More information about Role and Role Collections for SAP Build Process Automation is available here.
  • More information about Shared Environment rights is available here.

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation - Transition guidelines from Public Environment to Shared Environments
entry.content:
Dear Community,

In a previous blog post "How to transition to Shared Environments - getting started" we provided a getting started on how to transition from Public Environment to Shared Environment. Let's discover the "transition guidelines":

Is the project currently deployed on public environment?

  • If the project is not currently deployed on a public environment, you can simply create a shared environment and deploy.
  • If the project is currently deployed on a public environment and you don't need to keep the past instances (typically in a development tenant) you should consider:
    • Undeploy on public environment
    • Deploy on a shared environment and test the outcome
  • If the project is currently deployed on a public environment and you need to keep the past instances (typically in a Test or Production tenant) you should consider:
    • Deploy on a shared environment and test the outcome first
    • Stop using on public environment

Are you using "API Trigger" (*)?

Let's see the difference between a Process/Workflow deployed within a Public Environment compared to a Shared Environment:

  • When a process is deployed within the Public Environment, you can click on "Start Instance" to test your workflow, and quickly after the process is started
  • When the SAME process is deployed within a Shared Environment, clicking on "Start Instance" won't be possible and you'll end up with an error.

How to fix (**)...

  1. Open the corresponding process
  2. Add a trigger
  3. Select API Trigger
  4. Provide a name
  5. Your process contains now an API Trigger

How to test...

You can now click the "start instance"

How to call an API Trigger with Environment?

Calling an API with an environment means an extra parameter:

Example with an Action Project

Using your favorite API development tool, you'll need:

  • Add the environment parameter
  • Add an API Key
  • Test!

Development Tenant VS. Production Tenant

It's a good practice to create on both tenants the same environment id (identifier). The only difference between the two tenants would be the API Keys.

More information about:

  • API Keys - help topic
  • Processes and Workflows within Environment - help topic
  • Manage Destinations - help topic
  • Triggers - help topic
  • Add an API trigger to a project - help topic

entry.title: Build Process Automation Notifications Part 2 of 3 - Custom Notifications
entry.content:
SAP Build Process Automation is known as the go-to choice for low-code development of critical business processes and extensions on the SAP Clean Core, powered by AI. To this end, it is one of SAP's most customizable solutions. And since the release of the notification framework last September, customers have been clamoring for the chance to build their own notification templates.

We are proud to announce that that ability is now here!

Do you want to...

  • ... brand your organization's email message in your own voice?
  • ... insert custom variables to provide greater context at critical process decision points?
  • ... build and share familiar and recognizable templates for email, in-app or mobile across the company?

All of this is now possible with SAP Build Process Automation's new Notification Template Editor and first-class notification template artifacts.

Let's take a moment to go through some of these long-awaited, flexible new features.

 

Email, In-app and Mobile All-in-One Editor

The new Notification Template Editor in Process Automation treats any notification template as a single artifact, whether the notification is delivered over email, in an application, or to a mobile device. To that end, all of these versions of a notification template are editable in a single window, with convenient tab-based views.

 

Editing all possible channels for a notification in one place means that later, when end-users receive the messages, they can go to a personalization view and which type of notification they want for each event. You can be sure that the message they get over email is consistent with the message they get on their phone - no matter what their preference.

But the availability of different channels also means that you can tailor each one for its specific use case. For example, in the mobile tab, you can remove sensitive information that could appear on the lock screen of the phone. Sensitive business data can stay where it belongs: in the private view of the email body or an in-app message where a user is authenticated.

 

The Editor - A Standard Component

SAP Build Process Automation worked with the SAP Notifications service team to create the notification template editor as a standard component in the SAP Business Technology Platform library.

This means that Process Automation uses the same component that other SAP tools will use to edit notifications. Anyone who works on extending notifications will have the same experience whether they use Process Automation or other SAP tools.

Furthermore, future enhancements to the SAP Notifications template editor will be synchronized among all of the solutions that use this component.

 

Placeholders for Any Process Inputs or Variables

How can you provide a template that shows information about a running process, information that changes as the process appears, and then reuse that template in different processes? Or how can you re-use a notification template in two different phases of a process? If you use a delivery date as a variable, for example, how might you use the template before a product is delivered and the date is only an estimate, and also use it after the delivery when the date is concretely known?

The answer is by letting you map the placeholder to a different variable depending on where you use the template. The mapping view in SAP Build Process Automation lists all of the placeholders that appear in the notification template, and lets you pick static values, dynamic process inputs or variables to set the value when the process runs.

 

The mapping view in the Process Builder even warns you when some variables are unmapped, and then displays a success icon when you have resolved all the values. The tools guides you through intelligent and powerful reuse by making sure that you don't forget any steps. Your business users will always receive clear, relevant notifications with all available data filled in.

 

Reuse a Notification Template Anywhere You Send a Notification

So now you have created a notification template, added placeholders for concise are relevant process information, and adapted versions for email, in-app and mobile viewing. How do you pick where it will get sent?

Custom notification templates are usable wherever default notifications are possible. SAP Build Process Automation lets you send a notification whenever a Form or Approval step is created.

 

What It Looks Like to End Users

Notification templates would be useless if nobody read the notifications. The templates you make and pick in Process Automation are sent when your processes run to the recipients configured in your process. They arrive by email, on mobile, or for example under the bell icon in SAP Build Workzone (the "in-app" notification).

Even if the templates are customized, SAP Build Process Automation guides you to make consistent, actionable messages that concisely bring your end users right into your organizations critical processes, so that they can respond on time and keep your business running.

Links

  • SAP Build Process Notifications, part 1:
    • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/build-process-automation-notifications-part-1-of-3-default-notifications/ba-p/13868088

entry.title: SAP BTP Empowers Customers and Partners to Unlock Business Potential
entry.content:
SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) is transforming how businesses operate by providing powerful, AI-driven tools for development, automation, integration, data management, and analytics—across both SAP and non-SAP applications. With SAP BTP, businesses can harness the power of generative AI to streamline processes and innovate more effectively.

At SAPinsider EMEA 2024 in Copenhagen, Denmark, we had the opportunity to meet with some of the most innovative SAP BTP customers and partners. Their success stories showcase how SAP BTP is helping businesses break new ground with solutions like SAP Build, SAP Integration Suite, SAP Datasphere, and SAP Analytics Cloud. Here’s a look at some of these incredible stories.

SAP BTP Customers

  • Open Grid Europe (OGE) sped up HR process by 50% and improved invoice process efficiency by building applications with SAP BTP, using SAP Build.
  • Floralux unlocked 40% total cost of ownership (TCO) savings while gaining a global view of their data and customer insights with SAP BTP, using SAP Integration Suite.
  • Norsk Hydro accelerated development time, reduced data errors, and improved decision-making capabilities with the power of SAP BTP, using SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud.
  • Girbau modernized its analytics by providing a single source of truth to expedite decision-making with SAP BTP, using SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud.

SAP BTP Partners

  • SEIDOR empowers customers to stay ahead of the competition by innovating, extending, and integrating their data with SAP BTP.
  • REPLY brings together their strategic, creative, and innovative approach to drive transformative results with SAP BTP.
  • Avvale is driving modern business evolution through the circular economy with SAP BTP.

Discover more about SAP BTP including SAP Build, SAP Integration Suite, SAP Datasphere, and SAP Analytics Cloud.

Explore additional inspiring SAP BTP partner stories.

If you’re an SAP partner, access a wealth of exclusive resources on SAP PartnerEdge to further accelerate your growth and success with SAP BTP.

entry.title: Low-Code and No-Code Development with SAP BTP: Empowering Business Users
entry.content:
Low-Code and No-Code Development with SAP BTP: Empowering Business Users

In today's fast-moving digital world, businesses need to innovate quickly. But let’s be honest—traditional software development can be slow, expensive, and requires highly skilled developers. This makes it hard for non-technical users to actively contribute to building solutions. This is where SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) comes in, offering low-code and no-code (LCNC) development tools that allow business users to create applications with little to no coding experience.

What is Low-Code/No-Code Development?

Low-code and no-code platforms simplify application development by using visual, drag-and-drop interfaces instead of complex coding. These platforms help teams prototype quickly, bring ideas to life, and empower business users—sometimes called citizen developers—to take charge of their digital transformation.

How SAP BTP Makes Low-Code/No-Code Development Possible

SAP BTP offers several tools to make development easier for both technical and non-technical users:

1. SAP AppGyver – No-Code App Development

SAP AppGyver is a powerful no-code platform that lets users create web and mobile apps without writing any code. Here’s what makes it stand out:

  • Drag-and-drop UI builder for intuitive app design.

  • Easy integration with SAP and external systems.

  • Pre-built logic components to streamline workflows.

  • Cross-platform deployment, meaning apps work on different devices.

2. SAP Business Application Studio – Low-Code Development

For users who need more flexibility, SAP Business Application Studio provides a low-code development environment, offering:

  • Ready-made templates for SAP Fiori applications.

  • Seamless connection to SAP S/4HANA.

  • Extensibility with JavaScript and SAP UI5.

  • Cloud-native development with multi-environment support.

3. SAP Process Automation – Automate Workflows

SAP Process Automation combines workflow automation and robotic process automation (RPA) to:

  • Eliminate repetitive manual tasks.

  • Digitize approval workflows.

  • Boost productivity with automated processes.

Real-World Use Cases of SAP BTP LCNC Tools

Businesses across different industries are using SAP BTP’s LCNC capabilities to speed up digital transformation. Here are some practical applications:

  • Retail: Creating custom order-tracking apps without IT involvement.

  • Finance: Automating invoice approvals and reducing errors.

  • HR: Developing internal employee self-service portals.

  • Manufacturing: Building predictive maintenance dashboards for machinery.

Why Use Low-Code/No-Code with SAP BTP?

  • Speed & Agility: Faster development cycles mean quicker results.

  • Cost Savings: Reduces reliance on IT and expensive developers.

  • Empower Business Users: Enables domain experts to create solutions tailored to their needs.

  • Seamless SAP Integration: Works effortlessly with SAP S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, and other SAP applications.

Final Thoughts

SAP BTP’s low-code and no-code tools make application development accessible to everyone—not just developers. With these tools, businesses can innovate faster, automate processes, and drive digital transformation without the traditional barriers of coding.

Have you tried building an app using SAP BTP’s low-code/no-code tools? Share your experiences in the comments—we’d love to hear how these solutions are making a difference in your organization!

entry.title: Shape the Future of the Next-Generation Inbox
entry.content:
Attention My Inbox Users! 

Welcome to the future of task management with SAP Task Center, the next-generation inbox designed to streamline your workflow and keep you organized. If you use My Inbox within SAP Build Process Automation or SAP S/4HANA, your input is vital for making SAP Task Center truly exceptional. 

What is SAP Task Center? 

SAP Task Center is a unified inbox for task management across multiple SAP and non-SAP solutions. It aims to provide a centralized hub where you can manage all your tasks efficiently, ensuring nothing slips through the cracks. 

Why Your Feedback Matters 

As a user of My Inbox within SAP Build Process Automation or SAP S/4HANA, your feedback is crucial for our development process. By sharing your experiences - the good, the bad, and everything in between - you can directly shape the evolution of SAP Task Center. This collaborative effort ensures that the next-generation inbox is tailored to your specific needs and workflows.

How You Can Help

Participate in our survey and let us know what you think about My Inbox. Your insights will help us build a tool that truly understands and meets your workflow requirements.

Survey: Help Shape the Future of SAP Task Center

Together, we can make SAP Task Center an inbox that not only meets but exceeds your expectations. Your time and contributions are greatly appreciated.

If you’re curious to learn more about SAP Task Center, check out the SAP Task Center Product Page.

Thank you for being a part of this journey. Let’s build something remarkable together!

entry.title: Taking SAP Build Guided Experiences to the next level
entry.content:
Table of Content

  • An overview of guided experiences
  • Updated Features and Content
    • Restarting Guided Experiences
    • Multi-instance support in Guided Experiences
    • Collapsible Sections
    • Pre-built content for SAP SuccessFactors
  • Conclusion
  • Additional Read

An overview of guided experiences

In our last blog, we elaborated how Guided Experiences are designed to simplify multi-step business processes by breaking them down into manageable and intuitive steps. Powered by SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Build Process Automation, Guided Experiences can help navigate through complex business processes such as parental leave, benefits enrolment, employee relocation, new hire or supplier onboarding, training and enablement, or browsing and & purchasing electronic equipment. Users can model intuitive stages and stages which can by further enriched with content, documents, forms, UI cards and more. Please see our previous blog to learn more about modelling a Guided Experience directly in SAP Build Process Automation from a Guided Process Artefact, creating stages and steps within each stage , deploying to Work Zone and connecting Guided Process as Wizard template in SAP Build Work Zone. In this blog, we will elaborate on the recently released advanced capabilities like multi-instance support, restarting a guided experience, having collapsible sections and pre-built content from SAP SuccessFactors.

Updated Features and Content

Restarting Guided Experiences

One of the key updates is the ability to restart guided experiences. This feature allows users to reset and begin the experience from the start, providing flexibility and ensuring that users can correct any mistakes made during the initial run. The restart feature is also very helpful for easily demoing guided experiences. It allows presenters to quickly reset the process and demonstrate the guided experience multiple times without having to manually undo each step.
Note: this is available for both Work Zone only wizard layouts as well as guided experiences based on Process Automation guided processes.

Restart Button
The restart functionality is implemented as a button within the guided experience cover. When users click this button, they are prompted with a warning message that informs them that all progress will be lost, and the guided experience will start over from the beginning. 

Restart Guided Experience

A warning message is provided as a confirmation step, preventing accidental restarts / data loss and ensuring that users make an informed decision:
Warning message when restarting guided experience

Enable/Disable Setting
To provide further customization, the restart feature can be enabled or disabled per wizard. This setting allows administrators to control whether the restart option is available for specific guided experiences. Depending on the use case, restarting may not be applicable or feasible, in which cases this option can be disabled in the wizard settings.
Setting to enable restart

Multi-instance support in Guided Experiences

Another significant enhancement is the introduction of multi-instance support. This feature enables multiple instances of a guided experience to run simultaneously, allowing users to manage and track various processes concurrently. This is very useful for scenarios where multiple tasks need to be handled in parallel.Multi-instance overview for guided experience
Note: this is available for both Work Zone only wizard layouts as well as guided experiences based on Process Automation guided processes.

Use Cases
Multi-instance support can be applied to various guided experience use cases, including but not limited to:

  • Event Planning - Organizing multiple events simultaneously, each with its own set of tasks and requirements.
  • Purchasing IT Equipment - Managing multiple purchase requests for IT equipment, ensuring that each request is tracked and processed independently.
  • Grievances Requests - Handling multiple grievance requests concurrently, allowing for efficient resolution and tracking of each request.

Managing and Viewing All Instances
To facilitate the management of multiple instances, users can view all active instances in a dedicated interface. This interface provides options to:

  • Add Name - Assign a unique name to each instance for easy identification.
  • Restart Instance - Reset an instance to its initial state, allowing users to start the process over.
  • Cancel Instance - Terminate a given guided experience instance, removing it from the active list.

Multi-instance detail view for guided experience

Enable / Disable Setting
Similar to the restart feature, multi-instance support can be enabled or disabled per guided experience. This setting allows workpage owners to control whether the multi-instance option is available for the specific guided experience. Depending on the use case, running multiple instances may not be applicable or feasible, and this option can be disabled in the wizard settings in such a scenario.

Setting to enable multi-instance

Collapsible Sections

The collapsible sections feature is designed to improve the user interface for guided experiences and freestyle sections alike, providing flexibility for both enduser & content creators working with workpages.


Section Settings
Each workpage section comes with dedicated settings, including the option to make it collapsible. This allows users to personalize different sections based on their preferences and needs. Sections can be set to either expanded or collapsed by default, providing a tailored experience for each scenario. A typical use case is creating an FAQ page with many sections where the collapsed by default structure the workpage content nicely.


Note: this is available for both Work Zone only wizard layouts as well as guided experiences based on Process Automation guided processes.

Freestyle Layout
For freestyle layout sections, it is recommended to include a title or information for the dropdown. The title and information act as a header, making it clear what each section contains when collapsed.

Wizard Layout
In guided experiences, collapsible sections support various scenarios such as starting an instance, continuing an instance, and more. These collapsed sections also include a progress bar to visually indicate the user's progress of the guided experience. This feature is particularly useful for placing guided experiences space-efficiently on workpages without cluttering the interface. Example guided experiences in collapsed view:

Pre-built content for SAP SuccessFactors

We are excited to introduce pre-built content from SAP SuccessFactors, designed to streamline HR processes and enhance user experience. This content includes ready-to-use templates for employee onboarding and cross-boarding content packages, which can be easily deployed in SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Build Process Automation.

Content Availability
The pre-built content for Process Automation is available in the SAP Build Lobby store, while the content for Work Zone is provided as dedicated content packages via the Work Zone admin UI. These packages - among many other items - also feature a re-usable, configurable UI Card for item lists, allowing for a more personalized user experience for both users and content creators alike.

Scenarios of Content
There are three scenarios of pre-built content available:

  • Legacy (UI5 component card + workflow) - This scenario includes a single UI5 component card per guided experience combined with a workflow. It is however not recommended for new setups due to its outdated structure, limited flexibility & complex modification approach.
  • Work Zone Wizard - This scenario involves a Work Zone local wizard template, including UI cards. It provides a streamlined and intuitive experience for users, allowing them to navigate through processes with ease.
  • Process Automation version - This scenario is the combined version of Work Zone and Process Automation content. It leverages the strengths of both platforms to provide a comprehensive solution for managing complex processes.

For more detailed instructions on how to leverage this pre-built content, please refer to the following SAP Help guide. In this guide we also provide a detailed comparison table of those three scenarios of guided experience pre-built content.

 

Conclusion

The guided experiences are changing the way businesses can manage complex processes. From creating end-to-end onboarding experience for new employees to the application of parental leave, guided experiences are leveraging various capabilities within SAP Build as a deeply integrated, unified solution experience.

 

Additional Read

To learn more about the topic covered in this blog post, please also refer to the following assets & follow us on SAP community.

  • Related Blog Posts:
    • Guided Experiences powered by SAP Build
    • What’s new with SAP Build Work Zone guided experiences
    • SAP Build Process Automation - Introducing Environments
  • Learning Journeys & Certification: 
    • Work Zone: Implement and Administer SAP Build Work Zone | SAP Learning
    • Process Automation: Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation
  • Reference Architectures:
    • https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/refArchDetail/ref-arch-sap-build-work-zone
    • https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/refArchDetail/ref-arch-build-process-automation
  • SAP Help guides for more details:
    • Work Zone: How to Create a Wizard
    • Process Automation: Create a Guided Process
  • GitHub Repository for Work Zone Examples: https://github.com/SAP-samples/build-workzone-integration/tree/main/advanced/wizard
  • Video example Navigating Parental Leave with SAP Build Guided Experiences
  • 2024 Devtoberfest Guided Experience recording: https://www.youtube.com/live/q1pmO283Y5c?si=wfNm8eDNJPC0t_k8

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Build – Q1 2025 Release Highlights
entry.content:
SAP Build continues to evolve to support how modern teams work — now with more artificial intelligence, flexibility, and speed. In Q1 2025, we delivered a wave of innovations across SAP Build Code, Process Automation, Apps, and Work Zone to help developers and business users turn ideas into enterprise-ready solutions faster than ever.

Whether you're building custom apps, creating SAP S/4HANA extensions, or streamlining processes, these updates will help you move from concept to value more efficiently.  Let's review the top 10 updates we selected from last quarter.

Licensing and Availability Updates

1. SAP Task Center Now included with SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Task Center is now embedded in all SAP Build Work Zone editions, giving users a unified view of workflow tasks from multiple SAP systems — right within their Work Zone homepage. No separate integration is required. This update also extends to partner licensing, making including Task Center in custom Work Zone scenarios easier. The result is faster approvals, centralized task tracking, and higher productivity for business users and managers — all in one workspace.

2. SAP Build Apps Now Available in More Regions

You can now deploy SAP Build Apps in EU11 and Sydney data centers, bringing your applications to more users. The increased coverage improves app responsiveness, supports region-specific compliance needs, and enables global scalability for your enterprise use cases.

Joule for Developers Updates

3. Generate End-to-End Workflows with a Prompt

In SAP Build Process Automation, developers can now describe business scenarios in natural language — and let generative AI create forms, decisions, and process flows automatically. Powered by SAP's purpose-built large language models (LLMs), this capability delivers contextualized, SAP-aware outcomes that speed up workflow creation and reduce manual effort. It also empowers business users to contribute to process design without writing code.

4. Generate UI Pages from Data in SAP Build Apps

SAP Build Apps now uses generative AI to propose user interface pages based on your data model. For example, if your app contains entities like Books, Authors, or Genres, Joule recommends corresponding screens, layouts, and controls. This helps teams move quickly from data modeling to interactive UI design, shortening prototyping cycles and improving design consistency.

5. Use Generative AI for Fiori UI Creation in SAP Build Code

Developers can now use Joule in SAP Build Code to generate Fiori-based UIs for existing CAP apps by entering the command /add-ui or selecting the Joule icon in the Storyboard. Joule understands your application structure and proposes relevant layouts, controls, and navigation patterns, helping you scaffold front-end apps quickly and focus on customization instead of starting from scratch.

6. Multi-Agent Support for CAP App Generation

SAP Build Code now uses multi-agent support powered by Joule to generate complete CAP applications. Each agent focuses on a specific part — like data models, services, or UI — so you get a more complete project from a single prompt. This enhancement saves time and helps you focus on business logic instead of building everything from scratch.

Read also Overview of all GenAI Options in SAP Build Code by david_bizer.

Enhance SAP Business Suite Applications

7. Extend SAP S/4HANA with Custom UIs (Planned for Release Soon)

Coming very soon in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, developers will be able to use the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard to create side-by-side extensions—either by customizing existing SAP Fiori apps or building entirely new web applications. This approach enables teams to meet specific business needs while preserving the core system, supporting clean core principles and long-term agility.

  • See documentation: Using the Extensibility Wizard.
  • On setup, read the blog SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition by @Karthik-Thiru. 

8. New Enhancements for Guided Experiences by SAP Build

New enhancements to Guided Experiences by SAP Build help simplify complex processes like employee onboarding, parental leave, procurement approvals, and IT service management, where multiple steps, systems, and stakeholders are involved. With this release, ready-to-use content packages for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Onboarding and Cross-boarding are now available in the SAP Build Store—enabling faster rollout of consistent, step-by-step guidance for new hires and internal transfers.

  • To get started: Guided Experiences powered by SAP Build by florian_buech.
  • Watch the 2-minute video: Navigating Parental Leave with SAP Build Guided Experiences.

9. Intermediate Message Events for Real-Time Workflows

With new support for intermediate message events, SAP Build Process Automation lets workflows pause until specific API-triggered inputs are received and resume automatically. This powerful capability reduces wait times and manual monitoring, helping teams design more adaptive and responsive processes with fewer errors and overhead. 

Boost Developer Productivity

10. Revamped Project Overview in SAP Build Code

A redesigned Project Overview in SAP Build Code provides a clean, tree-based view of all app components, supporting CAP Java and CAP Node. This enhanced layout integrates with the Storyboard, making it easier for developers to navigate complex projects, onboard new team members, and stay focused while building.

Ready to See These Updates in Action?

These are just a few of the top updates rolling out this quarter. Want to explore the new features in more detail, see live demos, and get your questions answered by product experts?

👉Join us on April 15 for the What's New in SAP Build Q1 2025 webinar.
Reserve your spot to discover these updates in action. Register below:

📅There are two options to cover different time zones.
🕘9:00 – 10:00 AM CET → https://sap.to/604200bfg
🕔5:00 – 6:00 PM CET → https://sap.to/604500bfc

Learn More

For a complete list of new features, check out the following resources:

  • SAP Build Help Portal
  • SAP Build Road Map Explorer

Please stay current and connect with fellow SAP Builders by joining our Application Development and Automation Community for the latest updates, activities, and challenges.

entry.title: SAP Community Escape House for April – SAP Build 👷‍♀️🏠
entry.content:
The 👉 SAP Community Escape House has a new addition!!

We've built a garage for our workshop to build all kinds of stuff using SAP Build. Use your wits and knowledge about SAP Build – specifically SAP Build Process Automation – to try to escape.

WARNING: It will be really tough to escape – you have been warned! So I will be providing tips throughout the month to help you through, so subscribe to this post.

For this year, all SAP Developer Challenges created by the Developer Advocates will happen in the SAP Community Escape House (shout out to @nicoschoenteich for his amazing creativity). Each month we will unlock a new room in the house with a specific focus. Completing a challenge means successfully escaping the room by solving a series of coding – or no-code 😉 – tasks/questions. This month is all about one of the tools inside SAP Build.

Here are our previous challenges:

  • January: UI5
  • February: Integration Suite
  • March: AI

Visit the SAP Community Escape House and enter your numeric SAP Community user ID.

If you don't know your user ID, click Need Help? This will display a link to your community settings page.

Then you will see your ID here:

Once you see the SAP Community Escape House, select the SAP Build garage.

The user interface of an escape room includes the room itself as well as a terminal. You can click the objects in the room to receive tips.

Like in a real (physical) escape room, you will have to try out a few things and explore the room until you receive helpful tips and, eventually, instructions. These tips and instructions are printed in the terminal next to the room.

The terminal is also used to submit solutions to tasks. Read the instructions you receive carefully to understand how.

If you successfully escape the room (you will know when that happens), you will receive a badge in the SAP Community – and the unending esteem of your fellow SAP developers, business users and implementers. We will assign the badges once the challenge closes.

Good luck!

😺

entry.title: SAP Developer News April 3rd, 2025
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION 

Podcast: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2025/04/03/sap-developer-news-april-3rd-2025/ 

Developer Challenge 

  • Blog: TK 
  • SAP Community Escape House: https://sap-community-escape-house.cfapps.us10.hana.ondemand.com/house 

 Generative AI in SAP Build Process Automation  

  • Blog : https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-build-brings-generative-ai-to-process-automation-to-enable-your/ba-p/14049869  
  • SAP Help Portal : https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/generate-process?locale=en-US  

 Generative AI in SAP Build Apps 

  • Blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/create-sap-build-apps-applications-in-minutes-with-ai/ba-p/14054862 
  • SAP Help portal https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/generate-pages-using-ai 

 SAP BTP Usability Event – HANA Cloud 

  • Registration form: https://forms.office.com/Pages/ResponsePage.aspx?id=bGf3QlX0PEKC9twtmXka9zoyeoeObGdNh1zA5HmpzP5UMEVIN0xWUlBRWUlCWUlQUVJKV1pZMDFBWS4u  

  

CHAPTER TITLES  

0:00 Intro 

0:10 Developer Challenge 

1:12 Generative AI in SAP Build Process Automation 

2:08 Generative AI in SAP Build Apps 

3:08 SAP BTP Usability Event – HANA Cloud 

 

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for April 3rd, 2025.

[Daniel] You might have heard the sound of heavy machinery working on an addition to the SAP community escape house. Well, I'm happy to say that the SAP build garage is now ready. April's Developer Challenge will deal with a specific and key feature of SAP build. Well, I can tell you that. You have to discover that for yourself. It will include spy gear, exercise equipment, something from last month's AI challenge, and a few things that you won't see when you first enter the room. It will be a real challenge, no doubt about it. But if you're able to get out of the garage alive, please add a comment to my blog post and tell me how it went. Good luck.

[Rekha DR] Hello everybody! Are you a developer using SAP Build tools to build applications and automations? Then here is a good news for you to amplify productivity and efficiency in your day-to-day work. You can now use Generative AI in SAP Build Process Automation. You can enable Generative AI for your BTP tenant within the control tower of SAP. and then you can use Generative AI to generate a business process, a data type or a form from the overview screen of the project. Within the process, you can create an automation task, explain the business process in natural language. Generate new code in the script task, explain a decision or generate business rules. For more details, check this blog post by product expert, Yogananda Muthaiah provided in the description box. Keep learning, keep exploring. Thank you.  

[Shrinivasan] SAP applications are continuing to evolve with generative AI capabilities, and these capabilities are now available in SAP Build Apps. There is a new AI powered feature available in our no-code development environment now. SAP Build Apps now includes a generative AI feature that makes it easier to create pages for create, read, update operations on you. your existing OData backup. Simply enable an identity from the Integrations tab, and you will see the new Generate Pages option. Keep in mind, you will need to enable the Generative AI feature from the Control Tower first to use this option. Once generated, these pages can be customized and extended further to suit your specific needs. Note that the generated Pages feature comes with a usage limit of 100 requests per month for each user. Explore this new functionality to to jumpstart your application development. The links to the blog post and the help portal on this new feature is available in the description.

[Antonio] Holla, SAP Database Administrators and Database Developers. Have you ever wanted to provide feedback on the SAP HANA Cloud database service? Well, now you can. There will be an SAP BTP usability event on the 13th and 14th of May in Walldorf Germany. This is an in-person event where you can gain insights on the SAP's product vision. Also, you can share ideas directly with the development teams and provide your input on new features directly to the engineering teams. If you're interested in participating, make sure to register on the link that I'm going to share in the description of this video. Thank you very much for your time.Ciao

entry.title: New documentation available: Extend your SAP S/4HANA Processes with SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
"Intelligent Processes Are Integrated Processes"

If you're already automating and extending business processes on the SAP Business Technology Platform, you're likely thinking about the best way to connect your new process extensions to the relevant systems of record. This also includes SAP Build Process Automation, which serves as our central development environment for creating intelligent automations and orchestrating end-to-end business processes across complex IT landscapes.

The good news is that there is now a comprehensive new guide available on the SAP Help Portal to help with this process:

  • Extend and Integrate Your SAP S/4HANA Processes with SAP Build Process Automation

This guide walks you through the integration setup and management of SAP Build Process Automation with SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud. It is primarily aimed at system administrators and technical consultants who support customers using SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud and wish to extend their ERP capabilities by integrating with SAP Build Process Automation.

Covering the most crucial activities for successful integration, this guide is your go-to resource for connecting SAP Build Process Automation with SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

But first of all what is SAP Build Process Automation?

SAP Build Process Automation is a solution designed for citizen developers to adapt, enhance, and innovate business processes through no-code workflow management and robotic process automation.

It empowers both business users and technologists to take on the role of citizen developers. With its powerful, yet user-friendly low-code and no-code features, SAP Build Process Automation allows you to drive automation by leveraging the expertise of your citizen developer community. It allows you to:

  • Digitalize, extend, or adapt existing processes and orchestrate new end-to-end processes
  • Seamlessly leverage UiPath capabilities with SAP Build Process Automation add-on by UiPath
  • Build RPA bots to handle repetitive tasks so users can focus on higher-value goals
  • Accelerate document-heavy processes with intelligent document processing
  • Connect process and task automations to any SAP or non-SAP application

 

 What can you learn in this new documentation?

You should certainly bookmark this documentation to learn how to integrate your custom processes and automations with SAP S/4HANA, and extend standardized workflows to meet specific business requirements.

The runtime setup involves several configurations that must be executed in both the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) cockpit and the SAP Build Lobby. In this guide, we’ll walk through a common use case: pushing and retrieving business partner master data into an SAP S/4HANA system, whether on-premise or in the cloud.

Below, you can review the final architectures that you can achieve once you’ve completed the documentation. First, here’s the technical architecture for integrating SAP Build Process Automation with SAP S/4HANA on-premise:

 

Next, here’s the technical architecture for integrating SAP Build Process Automation with SAP S/4HANA Cloud:

 

 

Conclusion

It’s important to note that this documentation focuses on the integration between SAP Build Process Automation and SAP S/4HANA. However, the same approach can be applied to integrate with other SAP applications, such as SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Ariba, or SAP Customer Experience, as well as third-party systems. The setup can also be reused for processing various types of transactional or master data within a system of record. I hope this new asset will help you to simplify and standardize the connectivity o custom processes and automations to your existing backend applications.

entry.title: So this is it: I’m VERPed!
entry.content:
📢  Today is my last day working for SAP.

Indeed, I've been accepted into the very last round of the Voluntary Early Retirement Plan launched by SAP France in 2024, for which I applied the day of my 62nd birthday, on March 10.

It has been quick, but I'm happy. I thanks my management hierarchy Diana Roesner / André Borchert / Thomas Volmering / Steffen Pietsch / Michael Ameling / Muhammad Alam / Christian Klein (*) for giving me the unique opportunity to stop working 33 months earlier than expected! 😊

(*) yes, I was very low in SAP org chart 😉 but I do not care: what matters to me is to bring my expertise where and when it makes a real impact, and I humbly think it was the case during my 6.5-year tenure in this great company. 

I will really miss my wonderful team of such lovely colleagues in charge of SAP Build Product Management, Enablement and Communications 🥹  ... but I will still be hanging out here on SAP Community and on LinkedIn to follow what they will be doing with SAP Build and broadly SAP Business Technology Platform with Artificial Intelligence and Joule, because I know what will be announced in a few weeks at SAP Sapphire 2025, and for sure they will rock the stage!

 

I would like to heartfully thank Diana Roesner, André Borchert, Thomas Volmering, Michael Ameling, Sebastian Schrötel, Bhagat Nainani, Juergen Mueller and all the colleagues for having supporting me during my 6+ years at SAP, during this amazing journey from SAP Intelligent RPA to SAP Build. 🙏🏻

👩🏻‍🎓👨🏻‍🎓 I seize this opportunity to promote 4️⃣ learnings on SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP Build.

Indeed, I won't spoil anything but there will be tremendous announcements in a few weeks: leverage these learnings to be prepared!

✅ An Enterprise Architect’s View on SAP Business Technology Platform

✅ Experiencing End-To-End SAP Build

✅ Compose and Automate with SAP Build the No-Code Way

✅ Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation

📖  Let me also recommend the SAP Build Process Automation book from SAP PRESS!

Order this book now and learn how to create processes, automations, actions, and decisions by following step-by-step instructions with this expert-written guide by my dear colleagues @ArchanaShukla, @Chaitanya_Priya Puvvada, @Stephan Schluchter, Seshadri Sreenivas, and Steven Jacobson.

Finally, I would like to say that I'm very happy because I will spend 2 days in Paris April 14-15, 2 days in Walldorf May 12-13 and finally 2 days in Berlin May 14-15 to hang out with my colleagues and say a warm and grateful goodbye: if you will be there, come say hi! 🥲

And now, for me it's time to…

 

 💙 I wish you all the very best! 💙

entry.title: SAP Build 新功能：2025年一季度版本亮点
entry.content:
本文本为翻译文，原文档链接What’s New in SAP Build – Q1 2025 Release Highlights，原作者 Jesse Sukhbayar。

SAP Build 不断发展，利用更多人工智能、灵活性和速度，为现代团队的工作方式提供支持。2025 年第 1 季度，我们针对 SAP Build Code、Process Automation、Apps 和 Work Zone 推出了一系列创新成果，帮助开发人员和业务用户比以往更快地将创意转化为企业级解决方案。

无论您是构建自定义应用、创建 SAP S/4HANA 扩展还是简化流程，这些更新都将帮助您更高效地从概念过渡到价值。  我们来回顾一下从上一季度中选择的前 10 项更新。

 

许可和可用性更新

1.SAP Task Center 现在包含在 SAP Build Work Zone 中

SAP Task Center 现在嵌入在所有 SAP Build Work Zone 版本中，为用户提供来自多个 SAP 系统的工作流任务的统一视图 - 位于其 Work Zone 主页中。无需单独集成。此更新还扩展到合作伙伴许可，使自定义 Work Zone 场景中的 Task Center 更容易。这样，业务用户和管理人员就可以在一个工作区中加快审批速度，集中跟踪任务，并提高工作效率。

  1. SAP Build Apps 现在可在更多区域使用

现在，您可以在 EU11 和悉尼数据中心部署 SAP Build Apps，将应用程序提供给更多用户。增加的覆盖范围可提高应用的响应能力，支持区域特定的合规性需求，并为企业用例提供全局可扩展性。

 

Joule for Developers 

3.使用提示生成端到端工作流
